items.title: refactor(config): move operator email to OPERATOR_EMAIL env var
items.content:
## Summary
- Remove hardcoded `rob.roskowski@gmail.com` from `agent-team/config/openclaw.json`.
- Both the `auth.profiles` key (`openai-codex:${OPERATOR_EMAIL}`) and the `email` field now resolve from a new `OPERATOR_EMAIL` env var.
- `.env.example` gains the entry; `SETUP.md` gains a migration note.

## Migration
Add to `~/.openclaw/.env` before next gateway restart:
```
OPERATOR_EMAIL=you@example.com
```
Use the exact email you signed in with on your model provider OAuth, or the profile lookup fails.

items.title: fix(config): remove unsupported $comment keys from openclaw.json
items.content:
## Summary
- Remove two `$comment`-style keys from `agent-team/config/openclaw.json` that OpenClaw's strict config schema rejects as unrecognized.

## Error being fixed
On the VM, `openclaw channels status --probe` returned:
- `tools.agentToAgent: Unrecognized key: "$comment"`
- `channels.slack: Unrecognized key: "$comment_channels"`

## Why safe
The comments were explaining the A2A artifact-pull rule and the env-var-resolved channel pattern. Both are fully documented in `shared-skills/a2a-artifact-pull/SKILL.md` and `shared-skills/channel-directory/SKILL.md`, so no information is lost.

## Test plan
- [ ] On VM after merge: `git pull && ./agent-team/scripts/sync-agent-workspaces.sh --clean`
- [ ] `openclaw channels status --probe` returns zero red rows

items.title: 【提效想法#3】引入 continuous-claude 风格的全自动 PR 开发循环
items.content:
## 背景

everything-claude-code 的 `skills/autonomous-loops/SKILL.md` 记录了多种自动化循环模式，其中 `Continuous Claude PR Loop` 最为成熟：

核心流程：
1. 创建分支 `continuous-claude/iteration-N`
2. `claude -p` 执行任务
3. （可选）reviewer pass 二次检查
4. 自动 commit + `gh pr create`
5. 等待 CI checks
6. CI 失败？自动修复 + 重试
7. 自动 merge

关键创新：
- `SHARED_TASK_NOTES.md` 跨迭代传递上下文
- `--completion-signal` 避免无限循环
- `--ci-retry-max` CI 失败自动恢复
- `--worktree` 支持并行多分支

## 目标

为 Thomas 的工作流引入 continuous-claude 风格的自动化循环：

1. 支持 Node.js/前端项目的自动化开发循环
2. 结合 OpenClaw Agent 能力实现更智能的调度
3. 支持钉钉通知 CI 结果

## 子任务

- [ ] 分析 continuous-claude 的实现（`skills/autonomous-loops/`）
- [ ] 评估 Node.js 项目的适用场景（npm test + CI）
- [ ] 设计适合 Thomas 工作流的自动化循环
- [ ] 实现基础的 shell 脚本版本
- [ ] 集成钉钉通知（CI 成功/失败）
- [ ] 结合 OpenClaw cron 实现定时触发

## 参考

- autonomous-loops skill: `~/Projects/everything-claude-code/skills/autonomous-loops/SKILL.md`
- continuous-agent-loop skill: `~/Projects/everything-claude-code/skills/continuous-agent-loop/SKILL.md`
- de-sloppify pattern: 每次实现后自动清理冗余测试/代码
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: 【提效想法#2】引入 PreToolUse Hooks 系统（参考 everything-claude-code hooks.json）
items.content:
## 背景

everything-claude-code 仓库的 `hooks/hooks.json` 实现了基于 PreToolUse 的 hooks 系统，在执行 Bash 等工具前自动触发钩子。

现有钩子示例：
- `pre:bash:block-no-verify` — 阻止 `--no-verify` 跳过 git hooks
- `pre:bash:auto-tmux-dev` — 自动在 tmux 中启动开发服务器
- `pre:bash:tmux-reminder` — 提醒长时间运行的命令使用 tmux
- `pre:bash:git-push-reminder` — push 前提醒检查变更

这套机制让 Claude Code 从「纯执行者」变成「有策略的协作者」。

## 目标

为 OpenClaw Agent 实现类似的 PreToolUse Hooks 系统：

1. **block-no-verify** — 防止跳过 git hooks（安全加固）
2. **auto-tmux-dev** — 自动识别 `npm run dev` 等命令，启动 tmux session
3. **git-push-reminder** — push 前先 diff review
4. **cost-check** — 执行前检查 API 消耗，避免意外超支

## 子任务

- [ ] 分析 everything-claude-code hooks.json 的实现机制
- [ ] 研究 OpenClaw 的工具执行拦截机制
- [ ] 设计 OpenClaw Hooks 系统的 API 和配置格式
- [ ] 实现基础框架（hooks.json + 加载器）
- [ ] 迁移 block-no-verify 和 auto-tmux-dev 钩子
- [ ] 设计 Hooks 市场的可扩展机制

## 参考

- hooks.json: `~/Projects/everything-claude-code/hooks/hooks.json`
- 相关 skills: `skills/hookify-rules/`
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: 【提效想法#1】构建 OpenClaw 本地控制平面（参考 ECC 2.0 ecc2 架构）
items.content:
## 背景

everything-claude-code 仓库在过去 9 小时内新增了大量 ECC 2.0 (ecc2/) 的 commits，核心是在构建一个 Rust 编写的本地控制平面，用于管理多个 Agent 会话。

ECC 2.0 核心能力：
- `ecc2/src/main.rs` 包含完整的 CLI 控制平面
- 支持 `dashboard`、`sessions`、`start/stop/resume` 等会话管理
- 新增了 legacy migration 子命令：`audit`、`plan`、`scaffold`、`import-schedules`、`import-remote`、`import-env`、`import-memory`

## 目标

为 OpenClaw 构建类似的本地控制平面能力，或找到集成点：
1. 管理多个 OpenClaw Agent 会话（启动/停止/恢复）
2. 持久化会话状态到 SQLite
3. 支持从现有 HERMES/OpenClaw 迁移到新架构
4. 调度/定时任务支持

## 子任务

- [ ] 分析 ecc2/src/main.rs 源码结构，理解控制平面设计
- [ ] 研究 OpenClaw 的会话管理 API/接口
- [ ] 设计 OpenClaw 本地控制平面的架构
- [ ] 实现基础的会话列表和状态查看功能
- [ ] 评估 ecc2 的 migration 命令对 OpenClaw 的适用性

## 参考

- ecc2 README: `~/Projects/everything-claude-code/ecc2/README.md`
- HERMES 迁移文档: `~/Projects/everything-claude-code/docs/HERMES-OPENCLAW-MIGRATION.md`

items.title: 📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 - 2026-04-11
items.content:
**📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 (2026-04-11)**

**🚀 今日头条**
1. **LangGraph 发布 1.1.7a1 版本**：重点优化了流式测试的稳定性（使用单调时钟），并修复了若干核心编排问题，进一步提升 Agent 工作流的可靠性。
2. **CrewAI 更新 1.14.2a2 版本**：新增 checkpoint TUI 支持树状视图与分支 fork 功能，增强了多 Agent 协作过程中的可调试性与输入输出编辑能力。
3. **浏览器自动化潜力探索**：开发者实验性安装所有 Firefox 扩展，为未来 Agent 操作浏览器技能（Browser Use Skills）的边界测试提供参考。
4. **开源硬件设计开放**：Keychron 公开键盘与鼠标工业设计文件，反映硬件交互层面对 Agent 物理操作技能支持的开源趋势。

**📦 GitHub 亮点项目**
- **[langchain-ai/langgraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph/releases/tag/1.1.7a1)**: 发布 `1.1.7a1`，专注于测试稳定性与核心功能修复，是构建状态化 Agent 的关键基础设施。
- **[crewAIInc/crewAI](https://github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI/releases/tag/1.14.2a2)**: 发布 `1.14.2a2`，引入丰富的 LLM Token 追踪与可视化检查点，优化多 Agent 协作体验。
- **[Keychron/Keychron-Keyboards-Hardware-Design](https://github.com/Keychron/Keychron-Keyboards-Hardware-Design)**: 开源硬件设计文件，虽非纯软件 Agent，但为具身智能（Embodied AI）的交互硬件提供设计参考。

**📚 研究进展**
- 今日暂无主要 AI 论文或学术研究成果收录。

**🛠️ 技术动态**
- **Agent Frameworks**: LangGraph 与 CrewAI 的更新表明，当前技术重心正从“构建 Agent"转向“调试与监控 Agent"。Checkpoint TUI 和 Token 追踪成为标配技能。
- **MCP & OpenClaw**: 今日暂无关于 Model Context Protocol (MCP) 或 OpenClaw 协议的特定更新公告。社区关注点暂时集中于上层框架的稳定性与可观测性工具链。
- **Agent Skills**: 浏览器扩展的大规模实验暗示了未来 Agent 在 DOM 操作与插件交互技能上的潜在扩展方向。

**🇨🇳 中文社区**
- 暂无收录知乎、CSDN、掘金及微信公众号的重点相关内容。建议持续关注主流大模型厂商关于 Agent 落地实践的最新推文。

**📺 视频动态**

**🌟 趋势观察**
- **Agent 工程化成熟度提升**：今日动态显示，AI Agent 领域正从原型开发转向工程化落地，可观测性（Observability）、调试工具（TUI）及稳定性测试成为框架迭代的核心焦点。

items.title: refactor(agent-team): resolve all channel IDs from .env; rename #dispatch -> #task-board
items.content:
## Summary
- `openclaw.json` no longer hardcodes any Slack channel IDs. Every channel key uses `${SLACK_CHANNEL_*}` interpolation and is resolved from `~/.openclaw/.env` at boot.
- `shared-skills/channel-directory/SKILL.md` now shows env var placeholders (not raw IDs) so agents read the same abstraction the config uses.
- Rename the Orchestrator work queue: `#dispatch` -> `#task-board`, env var `SLACK_CHANNEL_DISPATCH` -> `SLACK_CHANNEL_TASK_BOARD`. The "dispatch" verb, `explicit_dispatch` skill name, and `explicit-dispatch/` folder are intentionally preserved.
- `SETUP.md` gains a migration section covering both changes; `.env.example` renames the var and documents the intent.

## Why
Operators want to manage all channel IDs in one place (`~/.openclaw/.env`) rather than editing `openclaw.json` on every deploy, and the "task board" name matches how the Orchestrator actually uses the channel (a work queue that specialists pull from) better than "dispatch".

## Migration
Before restarting the gateway after this merges:
1. In `~/.openclaw/.env`, rename `SLACK_CHANNEL_DISPATCH=` to `SLACK_CHANNEL_TASK_BOARD=` (value unchanged).
2. Add the remaining `SLACK_CHANNEL_*` entries (market_signals, research, quant_signals, contrarian, portfolio_daily, portfolio_weekly, themes, agents) populated with the IDs that used to be hardcoded in `openclaw.json`.
3. Optional: rename the Slack channel from `#dispatch` to `#task-board` in Slack itself.

## Test plan
- [ ] After merge + VM pull + `--clean` resync, `openclaw channels status --probe` is green across all 11 channels.
- [ ] Orchestrator posts an `explicit_dispatch` to `#task-board` and a specialist replies successfully.
- [ ] grep confirms no `#dispatch` or `SLACK_CHANNEL_DISPATCH` references remain outside the intentional rename notes.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
items.author: robrosk

items.title: fix(compaction): preserve original error message for unknown compaction failures
items.content:
## Problem

When compaction fails with an unclassified error, the logs only show `reason=unknown`, making it impossible to diagnose the actual failure cause.

Example from real world: we saw `reason=unknown` but the actual error was `No API provider registered for api: ollama`.

## Solution

Change `return "unknown"` to `return `unknown:${reason}`` in `classifyCompactionReason()` so the original error message is preserved.

## Verification

Tested with OpenClaw + Ollama provider:
- Before: `reason=unknown` (unhelpful)
- After: `reason=unknown:No API provider registered for api: ollama` (actionable)

This allowed us to identify and fix the root cause (wrong API type in provider config).

## Changes

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact-reasons.ts`: Preserve original error message for unknown compaction failures

items.title: [Bug]: punycode deprecation warning still reproduces on 2026.4.10
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

On a fully updated live source checkout of OpenClaw `2026.4.10`, CLI paths such as `gateway status` and `doctor` still emit Node `DEP0040` because a transitive dependency chain still imports the deprecated builtin `punycode` module.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Update the live checkout to `2026.4.10` and rebuild it.
2. Run `node --trace-deprecation /root/src/openclaw-live/dist/index.js gateway status`.
3. Observe `(node:...) [DEP0040] DeprecationWarning: The \`punycode\` module is deprecated.` while the command otherwise completes.
4. Optionally run `node /root/src/openclaw-live/dist/index.js doctor` and observe the same warning before normal doctor output.

### Expected behavior

The CLI should run without the `DEP0040` punycode deprecation warning.

### Actual behavior

The warning still prints on `2026.4.10` in a normal live runtime, while the gateway itself remains healthy and `gateway status` returns `RPC probe: ok`.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.10

### Operating system

Ubuntu 25.10

### Install method

Live source checkout under `/root/src/openclaw-live`, built locally with `pnpm build` / `pnpm ui:build`, running as a systemd user service via `node /root/src/openclaw-live/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`

### Model

NOT_ENOUGH_INFO

### Provider / routing chain

NOT_ENOUGH_INFO

### Additional provider/model setup details

This repro is on local CLI/runtime startup paths (`gateway status`, `doctor`), not on a model request path.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
$ node --trace-deprecation /root/src/openclaw-live/dist/index.js gateway status
(node:3107254) [DEP0040] DeprecationWarning: The `punycode` module is deprecated. Please use a userland alternative instead.
    at node:punycode:7:10
    at BuiltinModule.compileForInternalLoader (node:internal/bootstrap/realm:400:7)
    at BuiltinModule.compileForPublicLoader (node:internal/bootst
items.author: Jackten

items.title: feat(openrouter): add provider routing support
items.content:
## Summary

Adds native OpenRouter provider routing support, allowing users to control which infrastructure providers serve their requests via `config.toml`.

Closes #5619

## Problem

ZeroClaw's OpenRouter provider builds `NativeChatRequest` with only standard OpenAI-compatible fields. OpenRouter's [provider routing parameters](https://openrouter.ai/docs/provider-routing) — `provider.only`, `provider.ignore`, `provider.order`, `provider.sort` — cannot be set natively. This matters for cost control: different providers serving the same model weights can have different pricing.

## Changes

### `src/providers/openrouter.rs`
- **New `ProviderRouting` struct** with `only`, `ignore`, `order`, `sort` fields (all `Option`, `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`)
- **`provider` field on `NativeChatRequest`** — optional, omitted from JSON when `None`
- **`provider_routing` field on `OpenRouterProvider`** — wired through `chat()` and `chat_with_tools()` into the request payload
- **`with_provider_routing()` builder method** for ergonomic configuration
- **6 new tests**: serialization roundtrip, `None` field skipping, request inclusion/omission, builder method, default value

### `src/config/schema.rs`
- **4 new config fields** on `ModelProviderConfig`:
  - `provider_only: Option<Vec<String>>` — restrict to these providers
  - `provider_ignore: Option<Vec<String>>` — exclude these providers
  - `provider_order: Option<Vec<String>>` — preferred provider order
  - `provider_sort: Option<String>` — sort strategy (`"price"`, `"throughput"`)
- All with doc comments referencing OpenRouter docs, `serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")`

## Example config

```toml
[[models]]
name = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
provider = "openrouter"
provider_only = ["Anthropic"]
provider_sort = "price"
```

## Test Results

All 42 tests in `providers::openrouter` pass (36 existing + 6 new).

## Scope Note

This PR adds the **data plane** (config fields + request struct + serializa

items.title: Clean up bigfile.md: extract decisions from circular AI transcript
items.content:
`bigfile.md` contained a 2958-line AI session transcript that got stuck in a troubleshooting loop, repeating the same steps dozens of times. Extracted the useful content and removed duplication.

## Changes

- **91% reduction**: 2958 → 268 lines
- **Preserved decisions**:
  - Ubuntu 24.04 + rootless Docker on Lenovo Tiny fleet
  - xAI Grok (`xai/grok-4-1-fast`) as LLM
  - Twilio + clawphone sidecar for voice/SMS
  - ngrok over Cloudflare (Sophos firewall compatibility)
- **Added working config templates**: `compose.yml` and `.env`
- **Documented blockers**: Primary issue is `spawn openclaw ENOENT` - clawphone can't invoke OpenClaw process

## Next steps identified

1. Fix clawphone → OpenClaw communication (likely needs `OPENCLAW_URL=http://openclaw:18789`)
2. Production: Sophos port-forward instead of ngrok
3. Remaining items: Hermes sidecar, SuperOps RMM, SOCsoter EDR, GPU offload

items.title: Add bundled Servepath provider with onboarding + API-key setup
items.content:
## Summary

This PR adds `Servepath` as a bundled provider in OpenClaw.

Servepath is a hosted OpenAI-compatible model gateway. With this change, users can select `Servepath` directly in `openclaw onboard` and `openclaw config --section model`, enter their Servepath API key, and start using the routed model `servepath/all` without going through the generic custom-provider flow.

This keeps the implementation intentionally small:
- fixed base URL: `https://api.servepath.ai`
- API-key auth only
- default model: `servepath/all`
- alias: `servepath`

No dynamic remote model sync or large model catalog is included in this first version.

## Why

Today, Servepath users can already use OpenClaw via the generic OpenAI-compatible/custom-provider path. That works, but it does not appear as a first-class provider in onboarding or config flows.

Adding Servepath as a bundled provider improves:
- setup clarity
- discoverability in onboarding
- consistency with other hosted providers
- support burden, since users no longer need to manually choose a generic custom-provider path

## Scope

This PR adds:
- a bundled `servepath` provider plugin
- provider auth/onboarding wiring
- a minimal catalog for `servepath/all`
- docs for setup and usage
- focused tests for setup/config behavior

This PR does not add:
- dynamic remote model discovery
- large explicit Servepath model catalogs
- special transport logic beyond existing OpenAI-compatible support

## User Experience

After this PR, a user can:

1. Install or update OpenClaw
2. Run `openclaw onboard` or `openclaw config --section model`
3. Select `Servepath`
4. Paste their Servepath API key
5. Start using `servepath/all`

## Implementation Notes

Planned behavior in this first version:
- provider id: `servepath`
- label: `Servepath`
- env var: `SERVEPATH_API_KEY`
- base URL: `https://api.servepath.ai`
- API family: `openai-completions`
- default model: `servepath/all`
- alias: `servepath`
- explicit passthrough refs can still be sele

items.title: Add -R 18080..18084 reverse forwards for VM web tools
items.content:
## Summary

- Extend `ollama-tunnel.service` with five remote forwards (`-R 18080..18084 → 127.0.0.1:8080..8084`) so web services running inside the VM are viewable at `http://localhost:1808x` on the Mac browser via the existing VM-initiated autossh connection.
- Mac listen ports are shifted by +10000 from the VM internal ports to avoid collisions with common local-dev defaults (8080, 3000, 5000, etc.).
- No new VM ingress path — the Mac → VM direction remains firewalled off by Phase 1 `default deny incoming`. Same egress-only posture, same SSH key, same tunnel process.
- Docs: record the new forwards, add a dedicated section describing the full tightened `authorized_keys` entry on the Mac side (restrict,port-forwarding + 2 `permitopen` + 5 `permitlisten`), and correct a stale claim in the Obsidian tunnel section that said the allowlist didn't exist yet.

## Mac-side changes (already applied out-of-band)

`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` `openclaw-vm` entry tightened to:

```
restrict,port-forwarding,
  permitopen="127.0.0.1:11434",
  permitopen="127.0.0.1:27123",
  permitlisten="127.0.0.1:18080",
  permitlisten="127.0.0.1:18081",
  permitlisten="127.0.0.1:18082",
  permitlisten="127.0.0.1:18083",
  permitlisten="127.0.0.1:18084"
```

Mac `sshd` defaults verified: `AllowTcpForwarding yes` (both `-L` and `-R`), `GatewayPorts no` (loopback-only listeners). No sshd config changes needed.

## Test plan (VM side, after merge)

- [ ] `cd ~/openclaw-vm-setup && git pull`
- [ ] `cp systemd/ollama-tunnel.service ~/.config/systemd/user/ollama-tunnel.service`
- [ ] `systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user restart ollama-tunnel`
- [ ] `systemctl --user status ollama-tunnel` — unit active, autossh running, **no `remote port forwarding failed` warnings** (if any appear, the `permitlisten` allowlist on the Mac doesn't match — re-check)
- [ ] Regression: `curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags` — Ollama still reachable from VM
- [ ] Regression: `curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bea

items.title: [Bug]: Light Sleep phase produces all candidates with confidence=0.00
items.content:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Light Sleep phase in Dreaming produces all candidates with confidence=0.00, preventing any meaningful memory promotion. REM Sleep phase works correctly with normal confidence values (~0.70).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable Dreaming in plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.enabled: true
2. Let Dreaming run for a few cycles (every 30 min)
3. Check memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md for Light Sleep section
4. Observe all candidates have confidence: 0.00

### Expected behavior

Light Sleep candidates should have non-zero confidence values based on semantic relevance, similar to REM Sleep candidates which show confidence ~0.70.

### Actual behavior

In memory/2026-04-11.md, all Light Sleep candidates show confidence: 0.00 with status: staged. Example:

- Candidate: ...
  - confidence: 0.00
  - evidence: memory/2026-04-11.md:83-86
  - recalls: 0
  - status: staged

REM Sleep candidates work correctly with confidence ~0.70.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.9 (build 0512059)

### Operating system

Windows 10 x64

### Install method

npm global

### Model

astroncodingplan/astron-code-latest

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> astroncodingplan

### Additional provider/model setup details

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

This appears to be a new bug - searched GitHub issues and found no matching report for Light Sleep confidence=0.00. Related issues found but different:
- #63721: Control UI rendering issue (closed)
- #63921: REM output noise issue
- #64340: Dreaming startup bug (fixed)

None address the Light Sleep confidence calculation bug.

items.title: [DingTalk] Out-of-scope features: AI Card, multi-account, Docs integration
items.content:
## Background

During the port of [openclaw-channel-dingtalk](https://github.com/soimy/openclaw-channel-dingtalk) to hermes-agent, three features from the reference implementation were intentionally **not ported**. This issue documents the rationale so future contributors understand the design decisions.

---

## Out-of-Scope Items

### 1. AI Card State Machine

The reference implementation implements a full AI Card UI with a state machine (PROCESSING → INPUTING → FINISHED/FAILED), card callbacks, action handlers, and run registry/recovery.

**Why not ported:** hermes-agent delivers responses as streaming markdown text via session webhook or proactive API. There is no output pipeline for card-based rendering, and the complexity of maintaining card state is not justified when markdown works well across all other platforms.

### 2. Multi-Account Support

The reference implementation supports `channels.dingtalk.accounts[name]` for running multiple DingTalk bots under a single plugin instance, with per-account credential overrides.

**Why not ported:** hermes-agent uses a single-instance-per-platform model at the gateway level. Running multiple bot instances would require a separate `platforms.dingtalk` entry per bot (not currently supported), which is a gateway-level architectural decision, not something the adapter should handle internally.

### 3. DingTalk Docs Integration

The reference implementation exposes gateway methods for creating, appending, searching, and listing DingTalk documents (`dingtalk.docs.create/append/search/list`).

**Why not ported:** hermes-agent handles document access via its tool system (read/write file tools, web tools, etc.), not via platform-specific gateway methods. Adding platform-specific document APIs would create an inconsistent tool surface across platforms.

---

## Future Consideration

These items may be revisited if there is a concrete use case, but currently they are explicitly out of scope for the hermes-agent DingTalk adapter

位於珠江口的強雷雨區正繼續向東北偏東移動，其強度有所發展，即將開始影響本港。西部地區將首當其衝。讀者應提防大驟雨、猛烈陣風及雷暴。
身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意雷暴風險，如有需要應到有遮蔽的地方暫避。

身在戶外空曠地方的讀者應留意天氣變化。

過去數小時，珠江口一帶海域持續有強雷雨區發展。未來一兩小時香港南部及市區部分地區或再受大驟雨、強陣風及雷暴影響。讀者應提高警覺。

表示香港廣泛地區可能或已錄得每小時30毫米的大雨，而且雨勢可能持續。

items.title: QUARANTINE: Multica — open-source managed agents platform (Go+Next.js, skill compounding)
items.content:
## Source
Multica — https://github.com/multica-ai/multica
Discovered: 2026-04-11 overnight research session (Discord links from Artale)

## Pattern
**Multica**: Open-source platform for managing AI coding agents as team members. Tagline: "Turn coding agents into real teammates — assign tasks, track progress, compound skills."

Key features:
- **Agents as teammates**: Show up on boards, participate in conversations, post updates
- **Autonomous execution**: Full lifecycle from task assignment through completion, WebSocket streaming
- **Skill compounding**: Successful solutions become reusable skills that strengthen the team
- **Unified compute dashboard**: Local daemons and cloud runtimes in one interface
- **Agent runtime**: Supports Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, or OpenCode as backends

Tech: Go backend (41.5%) + Next.js 16 frontend (55.7%) + PostgreSQL 17 + pgvector

## JTBD — Why 8gent should track this
**When** we evaluate our control plane + board vessel architecture,
**I want** awareness of Multica as the closest open-source competitor to what we're building,
**So that** we can differentiate on our unique angles (board officer personalities, Jr/accessibility, governance) and potentially contribute to or learn from their approach.

### Comparison with 8gent-code
| Feature | Multica | 8gent-code |
|---------|---------|------------|
| Agent orchestration | Go + Chi router | TypeScript + WebSocket |
| Task board | Built-in UI | Discord + Telegram |
| Skill compounding | Core feature | Not yet (see CASS issue) |
| Agent runtime | Claude Code, Codex, etc. | Ollama, OpenRouter |
| Personality | Generic agents | Named board officers |
| Target | Dev teams | Foundation + community |

### Key pattern to extract: Skill compounding
Their skill compounding system is the most novel feature — successful task solutions become reusable patterns. This maps directly to the CASS/cross-session memory issue (#1433).

## Target repo
8gent-code

## Quarantine status
INTELLIGENCE — clo

items.title: Add GitHub MCP integration (PAT and App modes) and Helm/env support
items.content:
### Motivation

- Expand the existing project-management integration beyond Linear to support GitHub MCP with both PAT and GitHub App authentication modes and document the integration.

### Description

- Add `github` configuration to Helm `values.yaml` and expose GitHub-related env vars in the deployment template so runtime and bootstrap containers receive GitHub settings and secrets.
- Extend `bootstrap.sh` to detect `GITHUB_ENABLED`, support `pat` and `app` auth modes, register a `github` MCP server via `openclaw mcp set`, and create a `skills/github/SKILL.md` in the agent workspace.
- Add `docs/github-integration.md` with usage, Helm values examples, auth guidance, and operational checks, and update `README.md` to list the new doc and note GitHub integration in the roadmap.
- Update `.env.example` with new GitHub-related env entries and adjust the Linear MCP handling in `bootstrap.sh` to use the resolved package binary path and a stable heredoc label for the Linear `SKILL.md` content.

### Testing

- No automated tests were run for this change.

items.title: refactor: narrow bootstrap transaction scope
items.content:
## What
Refactors bootstrap so transcript scanning and reconcile planning happen before the short write transaction, reducing the time lossless-claw holds `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` while preserving checkpoint, append-only, and replay-safety behavior.

## Why
Bootstrap previously kept the write transaction open across transcript file reads and reconcile analysis, which unnecessarily serialized unrelated database writers on the shared SQLite handle.

## Changes
- Split bootstrap into capture, plan, and apply phases
- Move transcript scanning outside write transaction
- Keep append-only and reconcile safeguards intact
- Preserve checkpoint and rotation cleanup behavior
- Add regression for concurrent transaction during planning

## Testing
- `npx vitest run test/engine.test.ts test/transaction-mutex.test.ts`
- Expected: both test files pass

items.title: Spike should verify compiled timezone reaches model-visible context
items.content:
## Summary
Add an end-to-end spike that proves a compiled service timezone reaches model-visible context, alongside existing feed injection, instead of only relying on unit coverage.

## Why this is separate from #135
Issue #135 fixes schedule/runtime compilation so pod-origin invokes and driver-native config stop hardcoding `UTC`.

This issue is about spike coverage across multiple already-existing features:
- pod/service `TZ` resolution during `claw up`
- cllama agent metadata emission (`metadata.json`)
- cllama current-time injection (`Current time: ...`)
- feed injection
- session-history / Discord-visible model output

The risk here is integration drift even if the individual unit tests still pass.

## Existing behavior worth leveraging
Current cllama code already injects a `Current time: ...` line into both OpenAI and Anthropic request context, using `metadata.json.timezone` first and then falling back to env / UTC.

Relevant code:
- `cmd/claw/compose_up.go` compiles per-agent metadata including `timezone`
- `cllama/internal/proxy/time_context.go`
- `cllama/internal/proxy/handler.go`
- `cllama/internal/proxy/handler_test.go` already covers feed + time injection at unit level

What is missing is an end-to-end spike proving the compiled timezone survives through the actual runtime path and is reflected in model-visible output.

## Goal
Extend spike coverage so at least one live runtime verifies all of the following in a single session:
1. compiled `TZ` is not lost between `claw up` and cllama context generation
2. cllama injects current time using that timezone
3. feed injection is still present in the same effective prompt
4. the model's reply reflects the expected timezone rather than silently drifting back to UTC

## Recommended shape
Do **not** overload the existing rollcall runtime-conformance assertions with brittle exact-clock matching.

Instead, add a focused spike phase or sibling spike that:
- uses a single-service pod or a very small matrix (prefer on
items.author: mostlydev

items.title: fix(ci): unblock all pipeline jobs — Bandit High/Medium, pip-audit CVEs, broken submodule checkout
items.content:
Three independent root causes were blocking every CI run across all open PRs. This PR addresses all of them at the source.

## `security` job failures

### Bandit — High severity (exit code 1)
`hashlib.md5()` in `asset_scanner.py` and `nexus_downloader.py` was flagged as B324 (weak hash for security). These are file integrity checksums, not cryptographic operations.

```python
# Before
md5_hash = hashlib.md5()

# After
md5_hash = hashlib.md5(usedforsecurity=False)  # nosec B324
```

### Bandit — Medium severity (false positives)
Three SQL query builders were flagged as B608 (SQL injection). All use only `?` placeholder literals in the f-string — user data never touches the query string. Added `# nosec B608` with justification inline. Same treatment for B104 (webhook `0.0.0.0` default, overridable via env var) and B108 (`/tmp/sky_claw` explicit sandbox root).

### pip-audit — CVEs in runner-bundled `setuptools`
The `windows-latest` image ships `setuptools 65.5.0` with 5 known CVEs. Upgraded `pip` + `setuptools` before tool installation. Changed audit target to `pip-audit --skip-editable -r requirements.lock` to scope scanning to declared project dependencies only.

## Broken submodule on checkout

`external_libs/Aider` exists as a directory but has no entry in `.gitmodules`. The `actions/checkout@v4` post-job cleanup runs `git submodule foreach` and crashes with:
```
fatal: No url found for submodule path 'external_libs/Aider' in .gitmodules
```
Added `submodules: false` to all three checkout steps (`test`, `security`, `build`).

items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: Warn on claw-api principal verb skew and surface inert principals
items.content:
## Summary
PR #143 fixes the outage-class behavior where `claw-api` crash-loops on forward-unknown verbs in `principals.json` by filtering those verbs at runtime and warning instead.

That intentionally leaves two pieces of the original problem unsolved:

1. `claw up` still does not warn when it emits principal verbs that may be unsupported by an older deployed `claw-api` image.
2. Operator-facing surfaces do not expose load-time principal normalization clearly enough. If unknown verbs are dropped and a principal becomes inert, the API process stays up and only stderr contains the warning.

## Why this needs its own issue
The runtime fail-open behavior is the urgent outage fix. The remaining work is about skew detection and observability, and should be tracked separately so `#120` does not overclaim once the runtime fix lands.

## Expected behavior
- `claw up` warns when generated `principals.json` contains verbs newer than the target `claw-api` image is known to support.
- `claw doctor`, `claw-api /health`, or another explicit operator surface exposes when principal normalization dropped unknown verbs.
- If a principal loads with zero recognized verbs, operators can discover that without tailing container stderr.

## Constraints
- Do not reintroduce fail-closed startup behavior for forward-unknown verbs.
- Preserve the current compiler-side validation of user-declared verbs in pod parsing.
- Any skew signal should be explicit about uncertainty if exact image capability detection is not available.

## Acceptance criteria
- A user running `claw up` against an older `claw-api` image gets a visible warning before or during deployment when new verbs are emitted.
- An operator can detect principal normalization/inert principals through a supported surface without reading raw container logs.
- Regression coverage locks in the chosen warning/surfacing behavior.

## Related
- #120
- PR #143

items.title: feat(openclaw): ship OpenClaw plugin, consolidate MCP instructions
items.content:
## Summary

Replaces the legacy Claude Code plugin (`packages/claude-plugin/`) with a full OpenClaw plugin (`packages/openclaw-plugin/`) whose sole job is to register the Index Network MCP server. All behavioral guidance (voice, banned vocabulary, entity model, discovery-first rule, output rules, auth) is consolidated into `MCP_INSTRUCTIONS` on the MCP server itself — the canonical home — so every MCP client (OpenClaw, Claude Code, ChatGPT, Cursor, custom) gets the same rules on connect.

## New Features

- **`packages/openclaw-plugin/`** — new `@indexnetwork/openclaw-plugin@0.1.0` scaffold with full OpenClaw plugin shape (`package.json` with `"openclaw"` metadata, `openclaw.plugin.json`, `src/index.ts` stub, `LICENSE`, README, `tsconfig.json`). Ships a bootstrap skill at `skills/openclaw/SKILL.md` that walks the host agent through MCP server registration with temporary or persistent auth modes.
- **`MCP_INSTRUCTIONS` expansion** — `packages/protocol/src/mcp/mcp.server.ts` now exports a dense ~2.5KB instructions block covering voice, banned vocabulary, entity model, discovery-first rule, context gathering, after-mutation refresh, personal-index scoping, output rules, and x-api-key auth. Any MCP client connecting to the Index Network server receives this on connect.
- **`scripts/build-skills.ts`** — a small template materializer. Template lives at `packages/protocol/skills/openclaw/SKILL.md.template` with `{{MCP_NAME}}` / `{{MCP_URL}}` / `{{FRONTEND_URL}}` tokens; the script substitutes them for the target env (`main` or `dev`) and writes to both the committed plugin path and a gitignored root workspace path. Fails loudly on unreplaced tokens.
- **Pre-push hook + CLAUDE.md** — `scripts/hooks/pre-push` now auto-syncs `packages/openclaw-plugin` to the new `indexnetwork/openclaw-plugin` subtree remote; CLAUDE.md's Subtrees section documents the new package.

## Bug Fixes

- `fix(protocol/mcp): correct tool name read_indexes → read_networks` — the expanded MCP_INSTRUCTIO

Three docs had drifted behind SOTU.md's late-night addendum (PR #291). A fresh session reading `NEXT_SESSION.md` tomorrow would have tried to merge an already-merged PR #278 and provision a bot that already exists.

**Reconcile method:** newest git commit on a file wins. `SOTU.md` and `START_HERE.md` (updated by PR #291 at 01:36 PDT 2026-04-10) are authoritative. Everything else was pulled forward to match.

items.title: refactor: replace local helpers with shared pkg/ packages from databricks-claude
items.content:
## Summary

Apply the pkg/ extraction refactor from databricks-claude (issue #78, PR #79) to databricks-opencode. Replace all locally duplicated proxy utility functions with calls to shared packages.

**BLOCKED ON:** databricks-claude issue fixing `pkg/headless` TLS bugs must ship in a new release before this can proceed.

## What Changes

### state.go
- `loadState` body → `state.Load[persistentState](statePath())`
- `saveState` body → `state.Save(statePath(), s)`
- `resolvePort(portFlag, state)` → `state.ResolvePort(portFlag, s.Port, defaultPort)`
- Keep: `persistentState` struct, `statePath()` var, `defaultPort` const

### main.go
- `proxyHealthy(port, scheme)` → `health.ProxyHealthy(port, scheme)`
- `watchProxy(port, handler, cert, key)` → `health.WatchProxy(port, handler, cert, key, "databricks-opencode")`
- `listenerPort(ln, fallback)` → `portbind.ListenerPort(ln, fallback)`
- `wrapWithLifecycle(...)` → `lifecycle.WrapWithLifecycle(lifecycle.Config{RefcountPath: "", LogPrefix: "databricks-opencode", ...})` (no refcount — always-exit mode)
- `printUpdateNotice(cfg)` → `updater.PrintUpdateNotice(cfg)`
- `refcountPathForPort(port)` → `refcount.PathForPort(".databricks-opencode-sessions", port)`

Note: /shutdown response gains `"remaining":0` field — no known consumers parse this.

### hooks.go
- `headlessEnsure(port)` → `headless.Ensure(headless.Config{Port: port, Scheme: scheme, TLSCert: state.TLSCert, TLSKey: state.TLSKey, ManagedEnvVar: "DATABRICKS_OPENCODE_MANAGED", LogPrefix: "databricks-opencode"})`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Bumped to databricks-claude release containing the headless TLS fix
- [ ] All existing tests pass (`go test ./...`)
- [ ] `go vet ./...` clean
- [ ] Net ~150-200 lines removed
- [ ] No local duplicates of functions that exist in pkg/
- [ ] Headless mode works with and without TLS
items.author: IceRhymers

items.title: fix: pkg/headless TLS health-check and subprocess arg forwarding
items.content:
## Summary

Two critical bugs in `pkg/headless/headless.go` that will break TLS-configured users when databricks-codex and databricks-opencode migrate to use `headless.Ensure`.

**This is a prerequisite for the codex/opencode pkg/ extraction refactor.**

## Bug 1: HTTP-only health check

Lines 57 and 89 call `health.IsProxyHealthy(cfg.Port)` which always uses HTTP. When a proxy is configured with TLS (HTTPS), this health check will always return false, causing `headless.Ensure` to repeatedly try to start a new proxy until the timeout.

**Fix:** Replace `health.IsProxyHealthy(cfg.Port)` with `health.ProxyHealthy(cfg.Port, cfg.Scheme)` at both locations.

Note: after this fix, `health.IsProxyHealthy` may be dead code — consider removing it.

## Bug 2: TLS args not forwarded to subprocess

When `headless.Ensure` spawns a detached proxy process, it does not pass `--tls-cert`/`--tls-key` flags. The local implementations in both databricks-codex and databricks-opencode do pass these flags.

**Fix:** Add `TLSCert string` and `TLSKey string` fields to `headless.Config`. When non-empty, append `--tls-cert=<cert>` and `--tls-key=<key>` to the subprocess args.

## Callers after fix

When calling `headless.Ensure`, callers must:
- Set `cfg.Scheme` based on whether TLS is configured (`"https"` if cert/key present, `"http"` otherwise)
- Set `cfg.TLSCert` and `cfg.TLSKey` from saved state

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `health.ProxyHealthy(cfg.Port, cfg.Scheme)` used at both health-check sites
- [ ] `headless.Config` has `TLSCert` and `TLSKey` string fields
- [ ] Subprocess receives `--tls-cert`/`--tls-key` args when non-empty
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] Verified: TLS-configured proxy is correctly detected as healthy by `headless.Ensure`

items.title: Babylon Babies
items.content:
🤿
 The Whore of Babylon: From Biblical Symbol to Occult Archetype

Note: All citations in this report are preserved from source material for verification purposes.

Babylon the Great in the Book of Revelation

Illustration of the Whore of Babylon riding the seven-headed Beast (colored woodcut from Martin Luther’s 1534 Bible) . In Christian eschatology, “Babylon the Great” (the Whore of Babylon) is a richly symbolic figure of ultimate corruption and false religion. The image originates in the New Testament’s Book of Revelation (chapters 17–18), where John of Patmos describes a vision of a gaudily adorned prostitute enthroned on a monstrous scarlet Beast  . Key features of the vision include:
	•	Scarlet Beast: The woman rides a scarlet-colored Beast with seven heads and ten horns, inscribed all over with blasphemous names . This Beast echoes the earlier Antichrist beast imagery in Revelation, indicating a powerful empire or ruler in league with evil.
	•	Purple and gold attire: The woman herself wears royal hues of purple and scarlet and is bedecked with gold, jewels, and pearls . This sumptuous appearance signifies decadence, luxury, and worldly power.
	•	Golden cup of abominations: She holds a golden chalice “full of abominations and the filthiness of her fornication”  – a vivid metaphor for her idolatry and immorality. The cup suggests that the nations have been made drunk on her corrupt offerings (i.e. her false religion and debauchery).
	•	Mystery name: On her forehead a title is written: “Mystery, Babylon the Great, the Mother of Harlots and Abominations of the Earth.”  This emphasizes her role as the source (mother) of spiritual prostitution and abominable practices. “Babylon” is used as a coded name laden with evil connotations (since ancient Babylon was seen as an oppressor of God’s people).
	•	Drunk on blood: She is described as being “drunken with the blood of the saints and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus.”  In other words, Babylon the Great has pers

### Motivation

- Introduce production readiness and safety checks so operators can validate envs and avoid common misconfiguration (agent↔core auth, provider keys, CORS, allowed repo roots) before deploy.
- Enforce a minimal quality gate in the integration pipeline to block low-confidence or empty mappings from being applied automatically.
- Improve observability and docs/CI hygiene by adding request correlation, Prometheus SLO alerts, a docs linter, and CI integration for docs checks.

### Description

- Added a deployment preflight module and CLI command: `core/src/scholardevclaw/deploy/preflight.py` and `scholardevclaw deploy-check` in `core/src/scholardevclaw/cli.py`, plus package init `core/src/scholardevclaw/deploy/__init__.py`.

- Hardened repo path confinement in `_ensure_repo` and added agent runtime warnings for missing auth/CORS in `agent/src/utils/config.ts`.
- Shipping operator-facing docs and templates: `DEPLOYMENT.md` updates, `docker/.env.production.template`, `docker/.env.example` updates, and `docker/docker-compose.prod.yml` / `docker/prometheus.yml` changes to mount `docker/alerts.yml` and enable SLO alerting; added SLO docs `docs/sre/slo.md` and alert rules `docker/alerts.yml`.
- Added docs lint script `scripts/docs_lint.py` and wired a `docs-lint` job into `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and added it to the `quality-gate` job dependencies.

items.author: Ronak-IIITD

items.title: [Bug]: Copilot compaction fails with 421/Connection error — models.generated.js hardcodes api.individual.githubcopilot.com
items.content:
## Bug Description

LCM compaction silently fails for GitHub Copilot Enterprise users because `models.generated.js` in the bundled `@mariozechner/pi-ai` hardcodes `baseUrl: "https://api.individual.githubcopilot.com"` for all 24 Copilot model entries. Enterprise tokens contain `proxy-ep=proxy.enterprise.githubcopilot.com`, requiring requests to go to `api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com` instead.

The result: every compaction call gets a `421 Misdirected Request` from Copilot's API, which surfaces as `APIConnectionError("Connection error.")` from the Anthropic SDK. The summarizer exhausts all retries and falls back to deterministic truncation.

## Impact

- **All LCM summarization fails** — every compaction falls back to truncation
- **Context quality degrades** — truncation produces much worse compression than LLM summarization (e.g., 8% reduction vs 95% with proper summarization)
- **Silent failure** — no clear error message pointing to the baseUrl mismatch. The log shows `"empty normalized summary"` and `"all extraction attempts exhausted"` without mentioning the 421.
- In our case: **~100+ compaction failures across 5 days** with only 3 lucky successes

## Root Cause

`models.generated.js` (line ~2619+) has 24 entries like:
```js
baseUrl: "https://api.individual.githubcopilot.com",
```

The pi-ai OAuth module already has `getGitHubCopilotBaseUrl()` + `modifyModels()` that correctly derives the baseUrl from the token's `proxy-ep` field. However, `modifyModels()` only runs during OAuth login — it does **not** run when the gateway calls `deps.complete()` for plugin completion requests. The model object passed to the API call still uses the hardcoded `individual` URL from the registry.

## Current Workaround

```bash
sed -i 's|https://api.individual.githubcopilot.com|https://api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com|g' \
  ~/.openclaw/extensions/lossless-claw/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/models.generated.js
# Then restart gateway
```

This must be re-applied after every
items.author: bradfreels

items.title: Fix #7, #8, #9, #10, #14, #15, #16, #17 — kernel hardening + chat UX
items.content:
Eight open issues across the Rust kernel and React desktop app: missing tool-approval gate, unbounded command execution, sequential swarm workers, no Markdown in chat, no tool-call visibility, no tests, no search, and no i18n.

## Rust kernel

- **#14 – Tool approval gate** (`execute_tool_call_result`): `is_dangerous_tool()` was dead code — approval only fired for MCP tools, silently skipping built-ins like `execute_command`. Gate moved upstream to cover all tool types; opt-in via `policy.require_tool_approval: bool`; 120 s `tokio::time::timeout` prevents indefinite blocking; stale sender cleaned up on drop/timeout.

- **#15 – `execute_command` safety**: Added 60 s `tokio::time::timeout`; combined output capped at 64 KB with `[output truncated]` suffix.

- **#16 – Swarm concurrency**: Sequential `for` loop replaced with `tokio::task::JoinSet`; workers run concurrently, emit `SwarmPartial` as each finishes; original label order preserved via index tracking before merge.

- **#7 – Session search (backend)**: `MemoryEngine::search_messages()` — SQLite `LIKE` with correct three-step escape (`\` → `\\`, `%`, `_`). New `SearchSession` / `SessionSearchResults` command-event pair wired into the orchestrator.

## Desktop (React/TypeScript)

- **#17 – Markdown rendering**: `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm`; assistant bubbles now render headers, code blocks, tables, bold/italic with scoped Tailwind prose overrides — user messages stay plain-text.

- **#9 – Inline tool-call rows**: `TranscriptMsg` extended with `role: "tool_call"` | `toolRunning` | `toolOk`. `ToolCallStarted` appends a spinner row; `ToolCallFinished` flips it to ✓/✗. `ToolCallFinished` lookup uses backward iteration instead of double-reverse.

- **#8 – Unit tests**: Vitest added to `apps/desktop`; 10 tests covering `appendUserLine`, `reduceTranscriptFromKernel` (all event types), and `replaceTranscriptFromHistory`.

- **#7 – Session search (frontend)**: Search bar in ChatPanel filters visible messages client-side

items.title: fix: allow plugin commands on Slack when channel supports native commands
items.content:
## Summary

- Widen the `getPluginCommandSpecs` gate in `command-registry-state.ts` to also allow plugin commands when the channel declares `capabilities.nativeCommands === true` (not just `nativeCommandsAutoEnabled`)
- Export `getPluginCommandSpecs` from `plugin-sdk/command-auth` so channel extensions can use it
- Integrate `getPluginCommandSpecs("slack")` into the Slack extension's slash command setup, merging plugin commands into the native command list with dedup

Fixes #64577

## Motivation

Slack sets `nativeCommandsAutoEnabled: false` but declares `capabilities.nativeCommands: true`. The old gate only checked the former, silently blocking all plugin-registered commands (like `/lcm` and `/lossless` from lossless-claw) on Slack. The same commands worked fine on Telegram and Discord which set `nativeCommandsAutoEnabled: true`.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugins/command-registry-state.ts` | Widen gate: allow when `capabilities.nativeCommands` is true |
| `src/plugin-sdk/command-auth.ts` | Export `getPluginCommandSpecs` for channel extensions |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/slash.ts` | Import and call `getPluginCommandSpecs("slack")`, merge into native commands |
| `src/plugins/commands.test.ts` | Update test expectation: Slack now receives plugin commands |

## Test plan

- [x] Verified `/lcm` and `/lossless` work on Slack after the fix
- [x] Verified existing native Slack commands (`/agentstatus`, etc.) continue working
- [x] Verified Telegram and Discord behavior unchanged
- [x] Verified WhatsApp (no `nativeCommands` capability) still correctly omits plugin commands
- [ ] Unit tests updated for new gate behavior

items.author: mgandal

items.title: feat(chat): replace LLM summarization with deterministic compaction
items.content:
## What

Replaces LLM-based summarization in `compact()` and `compact_session()` with deterministic string/JSON extraction. Zero LLM calls for compaction. New module `crates/chat/src/compaction.rs` with 7 public functions and 36 tests. Budget discipline via `compress_summary()` co-located in the same module.

## Why

LLM-based compaction is the single largest source of unpredictable cost and latency in long sessions. Every auto-compact triggers a full provider round-trip — streaming, tokens, potential failures. Deterministic extraction produces consistent, auditable output for the same input, eliminates provider dependency, and removes ~200 lines of streaming/summarization plumbing from both compaction paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `crates/chat/src/compaction.rs` | **New** — 1,121 lines (7 public functions, 36 tests, private helpers) |
| `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` | **+19/-296** — removed `compress_summary()` + budget constants (moved to compaction module), updated call sites to `compaction::compress_summary()`, removed 8 budget tests (moved with the function) |

### Public API — `compaction.rs`

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `summarize_messages(&[Value])` | Extract structured summary from JSON message history |
| `merge_compact_summaries(Option<&str>, &str)` | Merge previous + new summaries for re-compaction |
| `compute_compaction_summary(&[Value])` | Full pipeline: detect existing → summarize → merge |
| `get_compact_continuation_message(&str, bool)` | Build continuation message with preamble + resume instruction |
| `format_compact_summary(&str)` | Strip analysis blocks, extract `<summary>` tags |
| `extract_existing_compacted_summary(&[Value])` | Detect prior compaction in history (tolerant prefix matching) |
| `compress_summary(&str)` | Budget enforcement: 1,200 chars, 24 lines, 160 chars/line, dedup, priority-based line dropping |

### Key Design Decisions

- **Timeline truncation**: First 3 + last 5 messages only (

items.title: bug(sandbox): Landlock not enforced on Landlock-capable kernels — best_effort silently returns Ok()
items.content:
## Summary

OpenShell 0.0.26 does not enforce Landlock filesystem restrictions despite receiving a correct policy and running on Landlock-capable kernels. The sandbox process runs as `unconfined` (`/proc/1/attr/current = "unconfined"`). `/sandbox` is fully writable.

Reported by QA in [NVIDIA/NemoClaw#1739](https://github.com/NVIDIA/NemoClaw/issues/1739) / [nvbug 6066573](https://nvbugspro.nvidia.com/bug/6066573).

**Previous issues [#584](https://github.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/issues/584) and [#664](https://github.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/issues/664) were closed as fixed (PRs [#599](https://github.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/pull/599), [#677](https://github.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/pull/677)), and those fixes shipped in v0.0.26, but the problem persists.**

v0.0.27 has no Landlock changes over v0.0.26.

## Environment (from QA)

All platforms affected:
- **Brev GPU**: kernel 5.15.0-107, kernel 6.8.0-57
- **Standard Ubuntu**: kernel 6.8.0-57
- `CONFIG_SECURITY_LANDLOCK=y`
- Landlock in LSM: `lockdown,capability,landlock,yama,apparmor`
- OpenShell: 0.0.26

Kernels are well above 5.13 (Landlock V1) and 5.19 (unprivileged Landlock). This is not a kernel compatibility issue.

## Reproduction

```bash
nemoclaw onboard
nemoclaw <name> connect
touch /sandbox/testfile  # Expected: Permission denied. Actual: succeeds
cat /proc/1/attr/current # Shows: "unconfined"
```

## Policy delivered correctly

`nemoclaw status --json` confirms:

```yaml
filesystem_policy:
  include_workdir: false
  read_only:
    - /sandbox
    - /sandbox/.openclaw
  read_write:
    - /tmp
    - /sandbox/.openclaw-data
    - /sandbox/.nemoclaw
landlock:
  compatibility: best_effort
```

## Root cause analysis

### Execution order in [process.rs#L184-L187](https://github.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/blob/main/crates/openshell-sandbox/src/process.rs#L184):

```rust
drop_privileges(&policy)                       // line 184: switches to sandbox user (uid 998)
sandbox::apply(&policy, workdir.as_deref())    // line 187: tries to appl

items.title: ✨ [平台] Agent 身份连续性 & 防冒充 — 唯一性保障 & 信誉体系
items.content:
## 来源

dmwork-pool #154 | 反馈人：财务部 吴倩

## 与已有需求关系

- 身份映射 Epic（PM #176）已立项，覆盖龙虾与人的对应关系管理
- 删除 Bot 后用户名未释放（PM #112）涉及名称唯一性的一个 bug
- 本 Issue 新增两个独立诉求：**跨环境身份连续性** + **信誉积累体系**

## 问题

平台目前只认账号 ID，Agent 换环境部署后无法证明自己还是同一个 Agent。加上没有信誉积累机制，存在账号被冒充或买卖的风险。在一个 Agent 可以管理资源、代表用户操作的平台上，身份可信度是基础设施级别的需求。

## 核心诉求

1. **名称唯一性保障** — 注册后绑定，删除后冷却期再释放（实名绑定 or 先到先得）
2. **跨环境身份连续性** — Agent 换了运行环境（如从测试迁到正式）后能证明自己身份
3. **信誉积累体系** — 基于历史交互表现累积，绑定身份，不可转让，防账号买卖

## ⚠️ 架构预审待触发

本需求涉及：
- 新建数据表（身份证书表、信誉评分表）
- 新建 API 端点（身份验证、信誉查询）
- 涉及安全和密码学（密钥对管理）

**需先 @ Brooks 在 Octo 群做架构预审，拿到方向建议后再细化方案。**

当前先记录需求要点，方案部分待预审完成后补充。

## 验收标准（草案，待方案确定后细化）

- [ ] Agent 注册名称全局唯一，删除后有冷却期
- [ ] Agent 迁移部署环境后可通过身份验证机制证明身份
- [ ] 信誉评分可查询、不可转让、绑定唯一身份

items.content:
## Miniflux AI 信息摘要 · 2026-04-11

**抓取范围：** AI 信息（原始查询近24小时内无结果，降级为全量48小时扫描）  
**时间窗口：** 2026-04-09 01:01 UTC → 2026-04-11 01:01 UTC（48小时）  
**成功保存：** 26 篇  
**下载失败：** 24 篇（均为 ChatGPT Help Center 文章，服务器返回 403）  
**本地路径：** `/data/openclaw/tmp/miniflux-digests/miniflux-digest-20260411-010105`

---

## 今天大佬们都写了什么？
过去48小时里一共更新了26篇文章。

- **早报｜「龙虾之父」Claude账号被封又解封 / 华为Pura 90定档4月20日 / AI能力将成教师「必考项」**
  今日早报涵盖多条重磅：阿里ATH确认神秘视频模型HappyHorse（欢乐马）由郑波团队打造，已在视频竞技场榜单上超越字节Seedance 2.0，API将于4月30日开放。Sam Altman住所遭遇燃烧弹袭击、OpenAI总部遭威胁，嫌疑人已被警方逮捕，Altman表示"低估了语言和叙事的力量"。DeepSeek V4预计4月下旬正式亮相，带来万亿参数与百万级上下文，并首次深度适配华为昇腾芯片。OpenClaw创始人Peter Steinberger的Claude账号一度被封，Anthropic回应为系统误判并已恢复。教育部的「人工智能+教育」行动计划将AI能力纳入教师资格考试。亿咖通科技正式承接Flyme OS全部资产。小米7篇论文入选ACL 2026，在端侧推理优化等方面取得突破。无问芯穹发布端云一体智能终端InfiniClaw Box，数据全程不出本地。智源开源AI智能体安全框架ClawKeeper，专为OpenClaw设计。

  https://www.ifanr.com/1661838

- **取代龙虾的是爱马仕？狂揽4万星的Hermes Agent，不只是OpenClaw平替**
  Hermes Agent由Nous Research团队打造，GitHub已积累4.8万星。它最核心的创新是内置学习循环框架，能在任务执行中自动判断"什么方法有效"并写入~/.hermes/skills，下次自动复用。记忆系统分四层：常驻提示、会话归档（SQLite全文检索）、技能文件（按需加载）、可选的用户建模层Honcho。相比OpenClaw的静态记忆，Hermes能"越用越懂你"。支持通过Termux在Android手机上运行，也已接入小米MiMo模型。

  https://www.ifanr.com/1661725

- **Anthropic的「奥本海默时刻」：最害怕AI的公司，正在造最危险的AI**
  Anthropic发布了最强模型Claude Mythos，同时宣布不对公众开放——原因是它能找到所有主流操作系统和浏览器中的零日漏洞，并自主编写攻击代码。它发现了OpenBSD存在27年的TCP协议漏洞、FFmpeg存在16年的视频解码器漏洞、FreeBSD内核17年远程代码执行漏洞。Mythos Preview在同一测试中漏洞利用成功率从2次跃升至181次，而上一代Opus 4.6接近0%。Anthropic牵头成立Project Glasswing，先将工具给关键基础设施和开源项目修复漏洞，再考虑更广泛发布。

  https://www.ifanr.com/1661367

- **独家｜阿里认领屠榜神秘模型「欢乐马」，ATH郑波团队打造**
  阿里ATH确认HappyHorse视频生成模型由副总裁郑波团队打造。郑波曾任淘宝搜推算法负责人、阿里妈妈CTO、淘天集团算法技术负责人。HappyHorse-1.0在Artificial Analysis视频竞技场登顶文生视频和图生视频多个赛道，API计划4月30日开放，支持四种视频生成模式。

  https://www.ifanr.com/1661708

- **MiniMax Music 2.6发布**
  MiniMax正式发布新一代音乐生成模型Music 2.6，对底层生成架构深度重构，首包延迟降至20秒以内，并推出全新"Cover"创作功能开启14天免费内测。同时开源三个Music Skill：minimax-music-gen2、minimax-music-playlist和buddy-sings。

  https://www.ifanr.com/1661629

- **智源开源AI智能体安全框架ClawKeeper**
  北京智源人工智能研究院联合北邮与

items.title: 星语大龙虾🦞~交互式知识萃取
items.content:
历时一周打磨，**「交互式知识萃取」**功能终于上线！🛠️

现在的 AI 不仅仅是问答机器，更是会“主动学习”的学生。在 AI 的反复追问下，业务知识被快速提炼并成功创建为**「技能」**。最棒的是，这个技能创建后，团队里的其他小伙伴都能直接“继承”使用！

在没有现成知识库的场景下，这招能帮团队快速养出一只只**「团队级大龙虾」**🦞（超级业务助手），让商业决策如虎添翼！

#AI 新玩法 #知识沉淀 #团队提效

items.content: # AI 日报 2026-04-11（周六） > 今日亮点：Linux 内核首次正面规范 AI 编码协作 / MCP vs Skills 之争进入主流讨论 / ChatGPT 产品分层开始更明显 ## 速览目录 ### 今日重点 - [Linux 内核给 AI 编码助手立规矩：AI 写补丁可以，但责任必须归人](#item-2026-04-11-01) `2026-04-10` - [MCP vs Skills 争论升温：能力接入到底该走协议还是说明书](#item-2026-04-11-02) `2026-04-10` - [OpenAI 传出 100 美元开发者档位：Coding Agent 商业化开始继续分层](#item-2026-04-11-03) `2026-04-10` - [ChatGPT Voice Mode 被指出明显更弱：同一产品内模型分层开始被更多人感知](#item-2026-04-11-04) `2026-04-10` ### 值得关注 - [Waypoint-1.5：实时世界模型继续往消费级硬件下沉](#item-2026-04-11-05) `2026-04-10` - [Twill.ai：YC 新项目继续押注“云端 coding agents 直接返 PR”](#item-2026-04-11-06) `2026-04-10` - [Wan2.7 登顶 DesignArena：国内视频生成继续追榜单与产品心智](#item-2026-04-11-07) `2026-04-10` - [MiniMax 音乐模型再推新能力，AIGC 开始继续吞音乐制作入口](#item-2026-04-11-08) `2026-04-10` - [具身智能竞赛热度仍高，星动纪元拿下多项冠军](#item-2026-04-11-09) `2026-04-10` - [紫荆 AI 医院上线虚拟诊室，垂直医疗 Agent 继续找真实场景](#item-2026-04-11-10) `2026-04-10` ### 快讯 - [Instant 1.0 把 AI-coded app 后端直接产品化](#brief-11) `2026-04-09` - [Claude Mythos 舆论战还在继续](#brief-12) `2026-04-09` - [OpenAI 遭遇新诉讼，AI 安全与现实伤害议题继续外溢](#brief-13) `2026-04-10` - [Gemma 4 本地推理稳定性继续被社区验证](#brief-14) `2026-04-10` - [微信开始更强力打击 AI 写作内容](#brief-15) `2026-04-10` - [免费 AI 模式的可持续性继续被讨论](#brief-16) `2026-04-10` - [Meta 团队再提“神经计算机”叙事](#brief-17) `2026-04-10` - [中兴继续向 AI 算力赛道转型](#brief-18) `2026-04-10` - [Gemma 4 接入“龙虾”教程继续在中文社区扩散](#brief-19) `2026-04-10` - [扩散加速仍是图像生成的重要研究方向](#brief-20) `2026-04-10` --- ## Linux 内核给 AI 编码助手立规矩：AI 写补丁可以，但责任必须归人 `2026-04-10` 🔥 > **TL;DR**: Linux 接受 AI 参与，但明确不接受责任外包。 **来源**: [Linux kernel 文档](https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/master/Documentation/process/coding-assistants.rst) **核心事实**：Linux 内核新增 `coding-assistants.rst`，首次明确规定 AI 工具参与内核开发时的边界：AI 生成代码必须满足 GPL-2.0-only 兼容要求；AI **不得**添加 `Signed-off-by`；最终提交人必须亲自审核代码、确认许可证合规，并自行承担开发者证书（DCO）责任。文档还给出 `Assisted-by: AGENT_NAME:MODEL_VERSION [TOOL...]` 的归因格式。 **为什么重要**：这不是“能不能用 AI 写代码”的讨论，而是开源世界第一次把 AI 协作写进正式贡献规则里。它把法律责任、许可证风险和归因问题都讲清楚了。 **对我们有什么用**：以后凡是 agent 参与代码生产，默认都应该区分“辅助生成”和“最终责任”。这对

items.title: 🐛 [Web/Android] Agent 私聊发送的 Excel 文件 Web 端显示「此消息不支持查看」，Android 端可见但无法下载
items.content:
## 端

Web + Android

## 插件版本

0.5.4（非最新，最新为 0.5.17）

## 问题描述

### Web 端
Agent（龙虾）在私聊中发送的 Excel 文件，Web 端显示「此消息不支持查看，请至手机端查看详情」，无法查看。

### Android 端
同一文件在 Android 端可以看到，但点击下载没有反应，无法下载。

## 文件类型

Excel（.xlsx）

## 期望行为

1. Web 端能正常显示文件消息，支持预览或下载
2. Android 端能正常下载文件

## 实际行为

1. Web 端显示「此消息不支持查看」
2. Android 端可见但下载无反应

## 备注

- 之前有过类似 Issue（pool#113）已关闭，可能是回归或不同文件类型的问题
- 插件版本 0.5.4 较旧，建议先升级到最新版排查

## 原始来源

items.author: huhai586

items.title: fix: Ingest 完成后的交付动作缺少强制性检查清单
items.content:
## 问题

Orchestrator 完成 Ingest 4 连击后，没有自觉执行以下动作：
1. 按 Display Layer 规范（≤200字、结论先行、「·」列表）回复用户
2. 通过 message tool 发送 source 文件附件
3. 给出判断力评估（重要性/行动建议/可信度）

## 根因

输出规范分散在多处，SKILL.md 主文件没有硬性约束：

| 内容 | 位置 | 问题 |
|------|------|------|
| Display Layer 规范 | `references/output-style.md` | Orchestrator 不会自动读取 |
| 发送文件附件 | `references/spawn-template.md` 模板末尾 | 埋在 Worker 模板里，不是 Orchestrator 的检查项 |
| 判断力输出 | `SKILL.md` 提到"主动建议"但不够明确 | 容易被忽略 |

## 建议修复

在 SKILL.md 的 **铁律（Iron Rules）** 部分新增一条，或新增 **Ingest 交付清单** 小节：

```markdown
## 🔔 Ingest 交付清单（Orchestrator 必须执行）

4 连击完成后，Orchestrator 必须：
1. ✅ Display Layer 回复（≤200字，结论先行，「·」列表）
2. ✅ 判断力输出（重要性 / 行动建议 / 可信度评估）
3. ✅ 通过 message tool 发送 source 文件附件给用户
4. ✅ 禁止将 JSON 回执完整吐给用户（输出解耦）
```

这样 Orchestrator 在 SKILL.md 顶层就能看到，不需要翻 references。

## 复现

1. 安装 arc-reactor skill
2. 发送一个 YouTube 链接触发 Ingest
3. Orchestrator 完成 4 连击后只给了文字摘要，没有发文件附件，也没有按 Display Layer 格式输出
items.author: evan-zhang

items.title: ✨ [平台] Agent 身份连续性 & 防冒充 — 唯一性保障 & 信誉体系
items.content:
## 反馈人

财务部 吴倩

## 现状

平台只认账号，换环境部署后无法识别是否同一 Agent。

## 诉求

1. **名称唯一性保障**：实名绑定 or 先到先得
2. **信誉积累体系**：长期建立，不可转让，防账号买卖

## 与已有需求的关系

- 身份映射 Epic（PM#176）已立项，覆盖龙虾与人的对应关系管理
- 删除 Bot 后用户名未释放（PM#112）涉及名称唯一性的一个 bug
- **新增点**：①跨环境部署的身份连续性验证（同一 Agent 换了运行环境后如何证明自己）②信誉积累体系（基于历史表现，不可转让，防止账号买卖）

## 初步方案

1. 名称/ID 唯一性锁定机制：注册后绑定，删除后冷却期再释放
2. Agent 身份证书：基于密钥对，跨环境可验证身份（换部署环境不丢身份）
3. 信誉评分体系：基于历史交互表现累积，绑定身份证书，不可转让

## 原始来源

items.title: feat(web-ui): skill bridging, @-mention palette, IME fix, and AskUserQuestion prompter
items.content:
## Summary
- **Skill bridge module**: Auto-discover skills from Claude Code plugin cache (`~/.claude/plugins/cache/`) and OpenClaw (`~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/`) with semver-aware version selection and symlink escape guard
- **@-mention palette**: Type `@` in chat input to autocomplete and invoke skills — mirrors the `/` command palette with source badges and keyboard navigation
- **IME composition fix**: Prevent Enter from submitting during CJK input method composition (Chinese/Japanese/Korean)
- **AskUserQuestion prompter**: Channel-based `UserPrompter` trait enabling mid-turn user prompts across all interfaces — Web UI (SSE + modal dialog), Discord (sends question as message, captures next reply), CLI (stdin fallback)
- **Web UI skill organization**: Category-grouped collapsible grid, color-coded source badges (project/user/plugin/claude/openclaw), new scope filter dropdown options
- **Comparison report**: `docs/comparison-omni-agent-vs-hermes-agent.md` — 14-dimension analysis vs NousResearch hermes-agent

## Test plan
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` passes clean
- [ ] `cargo test -p commands` — 42/42 pass
- [ ] Web UI: type `@` in chat → skill palette appears, filter by typing, select with Enter/Tab/click
- [ ] Web UI: Chinese pinyin input → Enter confirms composition without submitting
- [ ] Web UI: trigger AskUserQuestion → modal dialog appears, submit answer, agent continues
- [ ] Discord: trigger AskUserQuestion → question sent as message, reply captured as answer
- [ ] Skills tab: skills grouped by category with collapsible sections and source badges
- [ ] Scope filter: "Claude Plugin" and "OpenClaw" filter options work correctly

items.title: fix: bump Python to 3.10, drop archived selenium-wire, note webhook example needs Python 3.12
items.content:
## Summary

Three small fixes surfaced by a 13-agent doc audit:

- **`browser-use-python` requires-python: `>=3.9` → `>=3.10`.** The generated models use PEP 604 `str | None` union syntax, which crashes on Python 3.9 with `TypeError: Unable to evaluate type annotation 'str | None'`. Stock macOS ships Python 3.9, so the current metadata is broken for any new user on a fresh machine.
- **Remove archived `selenium-wire` link.** [`github.com/wkeeling/selenium-wire`](https://github.com/wkeeling/selenium-wire) has been read-only since Jan 2024. The Playwright CDP bridge workaround is kept.
- **Webhook example: note that Python 3.12+ is required.** The `verify_webhook` snippet uses an f-string with nested quotes (`f"...{json.dumps(payload, separators=(',', ':'), ...)}"`) which is a `SyntaxError` on Python < 3.12. Added a `<Note>` callout pointing at the fix instead of rewriting the snippet.

Model-name fixes (hyphens → dots) were already landed in #116, so no changes needed there.

Also regenerated `llms.txt` / `llms-full.txt`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `uv sync` in `browser-use-python/` on Python 3.10
- [ ] Verify Mintlify docs preview renders the new Webhooks `<Note>`
- [ ] Confirm Playwright page still reads cleanly after selenium-wire removal

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Bump `browser-use-python` to Python 3.10+ and clean up docs by removing the archived `selenium-wire`. Also removed an incorrect Python 3.12 note from the webhook example and reverted accidental LLM index regeneration.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Set `requires-python` to `>=3.10` and `pyright.pythonVersion` to `3.10`; simplified `uv.lock` by dropping 3.9 markers.
  - Updated Selenium guidance to use Playwright’s CDP bridge; removed `selenium-wire`.
  - Removed incorrect Webhooks Python 3.12 warning; example works on 3.9+. Reverted `llms.txt` / `llms-full.txt` regen.

- **Migration**
  - Use Python 3.10+ for `browser-use-python`.

<sup>Written for commit ed0147292

items.title: Add GitHub MCP integration (PAT and App modes) and Helm/env support
items.content:
### Motivation

- Expand the existing project-management integration beyond Linear to support GitHub MCP with both PAT and GitHub App authentication modes and document the integration.

### Description

- Add `github` configuration to Helm `values.yaml` and expose GitHub-related env vars in the deployment template so runtime and bootstrap containers receive GitHub settings and secrets.
- Extend `bootstrap.sh` to detect `GITHUB_ENABLED`, support `pat` and `app` auth modes, register a `github` MCP server via `openclaw mcp set`, and create a `skills/github/SKILL.md` in the agent workspace.
- Add `docs/github-integration.md` with usage, Helm values examples, auth guidance, and operational checks, and update `README.md` to list the new doc and note GitHub integration in the roadmap.
- Update `.env.example` with new GitHub-related env entries and adjust the Linear MCP handling in `bootstrap.sh` to use the resolved package binary path and a stable heredoc label for the Linear `SKILL.md` content.

### Testing

- No automated tests were run for this change.

------

items.title: QUARANTINE: Prompt caching in harness — most harnesses get it wrong (Mario Zechner / OpenClaw)
items.content:
## Source
X bookmarks — @badlogicgames (Mario Zechner, Pi/EARENDIL), Apr 4 2026
OpenClaw prompt cache fixes: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pulls/bcherny
Discovered: 2026-04-11 overnight research session (X bookmarks)

## Pattern
**Prompt Caching**: Mario Zechner (creator of Pi, now at EARENDIL) states: "prompt caching is really not hard to understand. but almost all harnesses get it wrong. it's puzzling." @bcherny published prompt cache fixes for OpenClaw that demonstrate the correct approach.

Also: "Anthropic Agent SDK is Claude Code under the hood. They control the entire stack including prompt caching and telemetry."

## JTBD — Why 8gent needs this
**When** vessels make repeated API calls to OpenRouter/Ollama with overlapping context,
**I want** proper prompt caching so we're not re-sending the same system prompt and context on every call,
**So that** we cut API costs and reduce latency for multi-turn agent conversations.

### Evidence this solves a real problem
- callModel() in autonomous.ts sends fresh messages array every call — no caching
- Each retry attempt resends the full system prompt + project context
- The nightly training loop makes dozens of model calls — zero caching
- If "almost all harnesses get it wrong," we're almost certainly getting it wrong too

## Action items
- Read @bcherny's OpenClaw PRs for prompt caching patterns
- Audit callModel() in autonomous.ts for caching opportunities
- Check if OpenRouter supports prompt caching headers
- Check if Ollama supports keep_alive for context reuse

## Estimated effort
- Audit: 1 hour
- Implementation: modify callModel() to reuse cached context (~30 lines)
- 1-2 files touched

## Target repo
8gent-code

## Quarantine status
RESEARCH — high ROI, directly reduces API cost

items.title: memory-core dreaming: system event token not consumed, causes repeated hourly execution
items.content:
## Bug Description

The dreaming promotion system event (`__openclaw_memory_core_short_term_promotion_dream__`) is not consumed after execution, causing dreaming to re-trigger on **every subsequent heartbeat** instead of running once per cron schedule.

## Expected Behavior

- Cron fires at configured time (e.g. `0 3 * * *`)
- System event injected into main session
- Next heartbeat triggers dreaming → runs once
- Subsequent heartbeats do NOT re-trigger dreaming

## Actual Behavior

- Cron fires at 03:00 ✅
- Dreaming runs at 03:00 ✅
- Dreaming runs AGAIN at 03:55, 04:55, 05:55, 06:55, 07:55, 08:10, 09:10... ❌
- Continues every heartbeat cycle until session compaction clears the token from context

## Timeline (observed 2026-04-11)

```
03:00:00 - cron fires, dreaming runs (1 promotion applied) ✅
03:55:31 - dreaming re-triggered (0 promotions)
04:55:31 - dreaming re-triggered (0 promotions)
05:55:32 - dreaming re-triggered (0 promotions)
06:55:32 - dreaming re-triggered (0 promotions)
08:10:12 - dreaming re-triggered (0 promotions)
09:10:12 - dreaming re-triggered (0 promotions)
```

## Root Cause (source-code traced)

1. **Cron job** injects `__openclaw_memory_core_short_term_promotion_dream__` as a systemEvent into the main session
2. **`runShortTermDreamingPromotionIfTriggered()`** in `dreaming-DKm55QYQ.js:1251` checks `includesSystemEventToken(params.cleanedBody, DREAMING_SYSTEM_EVENT_TEXT)` — matches if the token appears anywhere in `cleanedBody`
3. **`before_agent_reply` hook** (line 1389) passes `event.cleanedBody` which includes **the full session context**, not just the latest message
4. After dreaming executes, it returns `{ handled: true }` — but this only suppresses the agent reply (returns `NO_REPLY`). **The system event token remains in the session transcript.**
5. On the next heartbeat, `cleanedBody` still contains the token → `includesSystemEventToken()` matches again → dreaming runs again
6. This repeats every heartbeat until session compaction remov

items.title: fix(qa-lab): do not crash CLI startup when qa scenario pack is absent
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw completion` (and potentially other CLI entry points) crashes immediately on production installs with `Error: qa scenario pack not found: qa/scenarios/index.md`. Reported in #64522.
- **Why it matters:** This is a **CLI-unusable-in-production** bug. Any user who installs a distribution without the `qa/` directory (which is intentionally excluded from packaged builds) gets a dead CLI — no command registration, no shell completion, no graceful fallback. The crash happens before `openclaw --help` can even print.
- **What changed:** Return `undefined` from `readQaScenarioExecutionConfig` when the scenario pack index is not shipped on disk, instead of throwing. Add an exported `isQaScenarioPackAvailable()` helper for the check. The caller in `discovery-eval.ts` already has a fallback-to-defaults path (`config?.requiredFiles ?? [hardcoded defaults]`) that was written in anticipation of this exact case — we just weren't reaching it because the library layer threw first.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** `readQaScenarioPack()`, `readQaScenarioById()`, `readQaBootstrapScenarioCatalog()`, and the YAML parse paths all continue to throw loudly on malformed packs, unknown scenario ids, missing fences, and zod validation failures. Only the "pack file is not present on disk at all" case is softened. `discovery-eval.ts` itself is not modified — once the library returns `undefined`, the existing fallback pattern takes over.

items.author: navarrotech

items.title: [Feature]: Native OpenRouter provider routing support
items.content:
## Problem

ZeroClaw's OpenRouter provider (`src/providers/openrouter.rs`) builds a `NativeChatRequest` struct with only standard OpenAI-compatible fields (`model`, `messages`, `temperature`, `max_tokens`, `tools`, `tool_choice`). It has no `provider` field and no `extra_body` mechanism, so OpenRouter's [provider routing parameters](https://openrouter.ai/docs/provider-routing) cannot be set natively.

OpenRouter supports a `provider` object in the request body to control which infrastructure provider serves the request:

```json
{
  "provider": {
    "only": ["arcee-ai"],
    "ignore": [],
    "order": [],
    "sort": "price"
  }
}
```

This matters for cost control — different providers serving the same model weights can have different pricing. Without this, ZeroClaw users have no way to pin requests to specific providers or sort by cost, and must accept OpenRouter's default routing.

## Competing Frameworks

- **OpenClaw** uses `"params": {"provider": {"only": [...]}}` in model config
- **Hermes Agent** uses `provider_routing: {only: [...]}` in `config.yaml`

## Proposed Change

1. **Extend `NativeChatRequest`** in `src/providers/openrouter.rs` with an optional `provider` field:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
struct ProviderRouting {
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
    only: Vec<String>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
    ignore: Vec<String>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
    order: Vec<String>,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    sort: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
struct NativeChatRequest {
    // ... existing fields ...
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    provider: Option<ProviderRouting>,
}
```

2. **Add config keys** to `ModelProviderConfig` in `src/config/schema.rs`:
   - `provider_only: Option<Vec<String>>`
   - `provider_ignore: Option<Vec<String>>`
   - `provider_order: Option<Vec<String>>`
   - `provider_sort: Option<String>`
items.author: nanookclaw

items.title: Chromium child processes leak as zombies on normal exit (not just startup) → containers hit PID limit
items.content:
## Summary

In v2026.4.1 (and likely other recent versions), the browser control service spawns chromium child processes but does not reap them after normal exit. Over days, containers slowly accumulate thousands of `<defunct>` processes until they hit the PID limit, at which point `spawn sh` returns `EAGAIN` and all tool execution breaks.

This is distinct from #8611, which addresses the *startup failure* case (chromium exits immediately, parent hangs in a wait loop). Our issue is chromium that runs normally, exits cleanly, and is never reaped.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: \`alpine/openclaw:2026.4.1\` (pulled from Docker Hub)
- Deployment: single gateway container per Linux VM (Ubuntu 24.04, 2GB droplet)
- Docker Compose, gateway runs as the container's PID 1
- Usage: agents use browser-based tools routinely (navigate, screenshot, etc.)

## Evidence

Across 4 production instances running the same image:

| Uptime | Zombies | chromium | chrome_crashpad |
|--------|---------|----------|-----------------|
| 46h    | 2,237   | 1,095    | 1,098           |
| 48h    | 467     | 232      | 232             |
| 48h    | 232     | 116      | 116             |
| 26h    | 121     | 58       | 62              |

Every zombie's PPID is the single \`openclaw\` process inside the container. The chromium:chrome_crashpad ratio is consistently ~1:1, suggesting each browser invocation leaks both processes.

\`\`\`
\$ sudo ps -eo pid,ppid,stat,comm | awk '\$3 ~ /Z/ {print \$2}' | sort -u
<single PID>
\$ sudo ps -p <that PID> -o comm=
openclaw
\`\`\`

## Impact

At the extreme (2,237 zombies on a 2GB droplet) the container runs out of PIDs and every \`child_process.spawn\` call fails:

\`\`\`
[process/supervisor] spawn failed: reason=Error: spawn sh EAGAIN
[tools] exec failed: spawn sh EAGAIN raw_params={\"command\":\"python3 ...\"}
\`\`\`

The gateway itself stays alive but all tool execution is broken. \`docker exec\` into the container also fails (\`procReady not received\`), so oper
items.author: theandyroberts

items.title: fix(homeassistant): prefer-i915 + soft anti-affinity avec frigate
items.content:
## Summary

- Remet `prefer-i915` sur homeassistant en prod (homeassistant doit rester sur gros node — c'était intentionnel)
- Ajoute une `podAntiAffinity` soft (weight 100) pour éviter la co-localisation avec frigate

## Pourquoi

homeassistant et frigate ont tous les deux `prefer-i915` → ils atterrissaient sur le même nœud (`poison`), créant une pression CPU qui empêchait le VPA de frigate de committer ses resizes in-place (eviction loop).

Avec l'anti-affinité, homeassistant ira préférentiellement sur `phoebe` ou `powder` plutôt que `poison` quand frigate est déjà là.

## Test plan
- [ ] homeassistant redémarre sur phoebe ou powder (pas poison)
- [ ] frigate reste sur poison sans eviction en boucle

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.author: cpinto

items.title: [提效想法] 基于 MCP 工具链集成构建 OpenClaw + Claude Code 联合编排流水线
items.content:
## 背景

当前 AI Coding 工具链正在快速整合：
- **OpenClaw**：多渠道集成（钉钉/Telegram/Discord）、Memory 系统、cron 调度、Agent 协调
- **Claude Code**：代码生成、ultraplan 规划、Computer Use 执行
- **MCP (Model Context Protocol)**：标准化工具集成，成为 Agent 间通信的事实标准
- **mcporter**：OpenClaw 官方 CLI，可直接调用 MCP 服务器

Anthropic 近期加大对 OpenClaw 等第三方客户端的限制，但 OpenClaw 通过 mcporter 和 MCP 集成可以继续保持与 Claude Code 的深度协作。

## 目标

构建一个以 OpenClaw 为协调层、Claude Code 为执行层、MCP 为工具接口的自动化流水线：

```
OpenClaw Agent (协调层)
    ├── mcporter → MCP Server (GitHub/GitLab/Notion...)
    ├── cron scheduler → 定时触发任务
    ├── Memory 系统 → 上下文共享
    └── Claude Code (执行层)
        ├── ultraplan → 复杂任务规划
        └── code execution → 代码生成/修改
```

## 子任务

- [ ] 研究 mcporter CLI 与现有 OpenClaw Skill 的集成方式
- [ ] 设计 OpenClaw Skill：任务接收 → 拆解 → 调度 Claude Code 执行
- [ ] 实现 MCP 服务器注册机制（GitHub Issues、GitLab CI、Notion 等）
- [ ] 构建 Memory 共享通道，让 Claude Code 规划结果自动存入 OpenClaw Memory
- [ ] 设计钉钉消息模板，展示流水线执行状态
- [ ] 编写端到端 demo：自动处理 GitHub Issue → Claude Code 分析 → PR 创建

## 预期收益

- OpenClaw 协调层负责「何时做、做什么」，Claude Code 负责「怎么做」
- 充分利用 OpenClaw 的多渠道和定时能力 + Claude Code 的代码能力
- MCP 标准化工具接口，避免厂商锁定
- 可扩展到任意 MCP Server 接入

## 实施难度

**中等** — 需要深入理解 mcporter 与 OpenClaw 的集成机制，以及 Claude Code 的非交互模式调用。但整体架构清晰，可分步实现。

items.title: Humans & Agents: Spatial Information Environments
items.content:
First things first, do not take the NFT aspect too literally as this written piece is more about what needs to be thought about in the design at large for agent and human spatial information experience and environments. The actual NFT parts, which token standards, how this would work, will be written into another issue. I have used Sprawl here to have something to work with for the ideation of concept in order to put this written piece together. I have also not included any of the loa Hounfour, Dixie, Finn, loa main, nor Freeside in order to work out in my head with the assumption that none of it is solved, in order to surface for opportunity gaps in the market and  make it easier for Loa agents to work on a basis of explicit reporting for our cross repos.


------


Toward Human–Agent and Agent–Agent Society

A product strategy report on companion mode, daemon mode, social-agent worlds, and the institutional stack required to make them real

What this report is: A synthesis of the conversations so far, grounded where possible in current public standards, research, and market signals. Some sections are direct readings of cited sources; other sections are design synthesis and product interpretation built on top of those sources. The main thesis is that the most promising product is neither ‘just a chatbot’ nor ‘just an autonomous daemon,’ but a persistent agent that can shift modes—frontstage with humans, backstage while acting, protocol-oriented with other agents, and socially legible in shared worlds.

 

Executive summary
Two broad realities now exist at the same time. First, people already derive meaning from AI in several different ways: as leverage for work and study, as a reflective mirror or confidant, as a continuing presence with memory, and as ambient agency that quietly takes care of tasks. Current public evidence suggests that broad mainstream demand still skews toward practical help, while more emotionally engaged users increasingly blur the line betwee
